channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: In parts of Assam, election candidates uphold courtesy despite heated campaigning
item.description: The 2026 Assam Assembly elections have witnessed several constituencies where political competition remains grounded in respect, even as the broader campaign across the State has often turned aggressi...

item.title: Deputy Speaker Kalu Condemns Deadly Attack in Plateau, Urges Action to Protect Lives
item.description: The Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives, Benjamin Kalu, don sharply condemn the recent attack wey happen for Angwan Rukuba inside Jos North Local Government Area. The attack, wey take place...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Outlines Austerity and Relief Measures Amid Global Fuel Crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has assured that the government will continue efforts to extend financial relief to Pakistan’s poor and middle-income citizens despite the ongoing global fuel crisis. Dur...

item.title: Telposta Pension Scheme to Sell Properties to Improve Pension Liquidity
item.description: The Telposta Pension Scheme (TPS) has set in motion a plan to sell part of its extensive property portfolio in order to improve cash flow and ensure timely pension payments to its members. Currently, ...

item.title: NBC hosts forum to strengthen women entrepreneurship in Dar es Salaam
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM: National Bank of Commerce (NBC) recently organized a powerful forum for women traders and entrepreneurs in Dar es Salaam as part of its 'Women’s Month' activities celebrating Internatio...

item.title: Lahore Qalandars fines Shaheen Afridi Rs1m for hotel security misunderstanding
item.description: Lahore Qalandars, a Pakistan Super League franchise, has imposed a voluntary fine of Rs1 million on their captain, fast bowler Shaheen Shah Afridi, following an incident of alleged security protocol b...

item.title: NASA outlines phased plan for a sustainable lunar base by the 2030s
item.description: NASA has updated its Artemis program with a focus on establishing a sustainable human presence on the Moon rather than just achieving milestones. Unlike the Apollo missions, which were brief landings,...

item.title: Masood Azhar’s elder brother reportedly passes away, circulated on Jaish-linked channels
item.description: On Monday, several pro-Jaish-e-Mohammed Telegram channels shared news about the passing of Maulana Masood Azhar’s elder brother, Hakim Mohammad Tahir Anwar. The exact cause of his death has not been d...

item.title: Full List of Victims from Kaduna Wedding Attack Released by Authorities
item.description: Na wa o, this matter serious well. A full list don come out for the people wey die, wound, or get carried away during that terrible attack for a wedding inside Southern Kaduna. The sad incident happen...

item.title: Police Intensify Search for Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja Over Senate Committee Absence
item.description: The police have stepped up efforts to trace Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja after he failed to appear before the Senate County Public Accounts Committee (CPAC). Regional Police Commander Issa Mohamud ...

item.title: Security Sources Warn of Possible Indian False Flag Operation Involving Pakistani Prisoners
item.description: According to security sources, India is reportedly preparing a false flag operation targeted against Pakistan by allegedly using Pakistani prisoners who had crossed the border mistakenly and are curre...

item.title: Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie absent from Windsor Easter service amid family controversies
item.description: Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie, daughters of Andrew, Duke of York, and Sarah, Duchess of York, will not be attending the Royal Family's traditional Easter Sunday church service at St George's Chapel,...

item.title: Trump eases stance on Cuba as Russia delivers oil amid blockade tensions
item.description: Russia has delivered an oil tanker carrying around 100,000 metric tonnes of crude to Cuba, marking the first such shipment in three months amid a severe energy crisis on the island. Despite maintainin...

item.title: Iran launches missile at Turkey intercepted by Nato amid escalating conflict
item.description: On 30 March 2026, Iran fired a ballistic missile into Turkish airspace, which was successfully intercepted by Nato air defences in the eastern Mediterranean. This represents the fourth such incident s...

item.title: US judge allows lawsuits against Colgate over child safety labelling on fluoride mouthwash
item.description: A United States federal judge has authorised two class-action lawsuits against Colgate-Palmolive, accusing the company of deceiving parents about the suitability of its fluoride-based mouthwashes for ...

item.title: PCB fines Naseem Shah Rs20 million over central contract breach
item.description: Lahore: The Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) on Monday imposed a fine of Rs20 million on fast bowler Naseem Shah after he breached several terms of his central contract. The show-cause notice was initiall...

item.title: Senate Committee Orders Arrest of Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja Over Accountability Issues
item.description: Kenya’s law enforcement agencies are now seeking the arrest of Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja following a directive issued by a Senate watchdog committee last week. The committee’s decision reportedl...

item.title: Comorian Speaker Strengthens Parliamentary Ties with Tanzania, Invites Official Visit
item.description: The Tanzanian Ambassador to Comoros, Saidi Yakubu, concluded his service in Comoros with a formal farewell meeting with the Speaker of the Assembly of the Union of the Comoros, Moustadroine Abdou. Amb...

item.title: Metro warehouse and head office staff in Quebec start strike over pay and working conditions
item.description: On Monday morning, workers at Metro’s MRU-T head office and the Laval fruit and vegetable distribution centre in Quebec initiated a strike affecting approximately 550 warehouse employees and drivers a...

item.title: Peel Police Investigate Historical Abuse Allegations at Closed Robert Land Academy
item.description: Peel Regional Police have launched an investigation into allegations of child abuse at Robert Land Academy, a former military-style private boarding school for boys in Ontario's Niagara Region that op...
